comment.body:
for the sake of simplicity:
you have long gamma at 6850
and negative speed above (shorter gamma upward), then a sudden long gamma at 6950
(the net speed profile is still negative lets say, bcs the long gamma at 6950 is less than the long gamma at 6850)

comment.body:
so in the first case when speed was negative between the two long gamma levels 6850 - 6950
the market left a gap behind even if it does not appear on the charts as it was made during RTH,
local liquidity was formed at 6950 (artificial liquidity),
but the gap below acts as a vacuum, with a magnet downward, as speed is negative, vol is sold downward.
comment.raw_body:
so in the first case when speed was negative between the two long gamma levels 6850 - 6950
the market left a gap behind even if it does not appear on the charts as it was made during RTH,
local liquidity was formed at 6950 (artificial liquidity),
but the gap below acts as a vacuum, with a magnet downward, as speed is negative, vol is sold downward.

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
in the second case, when speed profile was positive,
the market expected liquidity upward, so the gap below act as a simple illiquid zone that can be retested, but the stability, the "magnet", the 'liquidity" is at 6950, not 6850
liquidity moved from 6850 to 6950

comment.body:
key takeaway: 
be proactive,
be adaptive,
don't try to tell what the market wants to do, let it show you what it wants to do, and ride the wave,
and MAKE PLAN!!! :)))

and a pro tip may do not take serious:
use paper trading account for practicing. you won't miss out anything, if you practice for months
you will just be better
